archimate:BusinessObject.name: Interoperability Policy
archimate:BusinessObject.documentation:
Definition: An Interoperability Policy is a set of guidelines that outlines how different systems and data should work together seamlessly within an organization or across entities. It ensures that data formats, protocols, and interfaces are compatible and consistent, enabling effective data sharing and collaboration. The policy promotes the use of open standards and best practices to facilitate smooth communication and integration between various systems, applications, and stakeholders.
Source: EIF
Source reference: https://ec.europa.eu/isa2/sites/isa/files/eif_brochure_final.pdf
LOST view: SV-Governance Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/InteroperabilityPolicyBusinessObject
ABB name: egovera:InteroperabilityPolicyBusinessObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/InteroperabilityPolicyBusinessObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataPolicy

properties.value: An Interoperability Policy is a set of guidelines that outlines how different systems and data should work together seamlessly within an organization or across entities. It ensures that data formats, protocols, and interfaces are compatible and consistent, enabling effective data sharing and collaboration. The policy promotes the use of open standards and best practices to facilitate smooth communication and integration between various systems, applications, and stakeholders.
